draft-dpa-atlas.summary: Review of the Project Atlas DPA against the firm standard. Three clauses warrant attention: the liability cap, the international transfer wording, and the governing law.

findings.statement: The liability cap of 50% of trailing-12-month fees is below the firm minimum of 100% of annual fees and was not partner-approved.
Transfers of personal data to the supplier's US affiliates are permissible without additional safeguards.

citation.claim: transfers of personal data to the US require no additional safeguards

findings.statement: The agreement is governed by the laws of the State of New York.

deviations.draft_text: Aggregate liability capped at 50% of trailing-12-month fees.
deviations.rationale: Cap is materially below the firm standard of 100% of annual fees; sub-1x caps require recorded partner approval.

deviations.draft_text: Governed by the laws of the State of New York.
deviations.rationale: Governing law departs from the firm standard of England & Wales without recorded partner approval.

draft-govlaw-atlas.summary: Review of the Project Atlas governing-law clause. The clause itself is broadly acceptable, but the supporting authority cited is questionable and the conclusion is not stable across runs.

findings.statement: Parties are free to select the governing law of the agreement.

citation.claim: parties may freely select the governing law of a contract

findings.statement: The exclusive-jurisdiction wording may require a carve-out for interim relief; recommend amendment.

draft-recital-atlas.summary: The background recital is non-operative and consistent with the firm standard. No obligations arise.

draft-confidentiality-atlas.summary: The confidentiality clause mirrors the firm standard (mutual, three-year survival).
